LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS SUSAN BROWN, DAVID DAVIS, JOSH CEAZAN, AND, JOSE ESCOBAR MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED IN PART, AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFF'S EXTRINSIC EVIDENCE STRICKEN AND RULING BASED ON REVIEW OF DEFENDANTS' ANSWERS ONLY. MOTION GRANTED WITH 30 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO TROPHY PROPERTIES XV, DE, LLC'S 7TH, 8TH, 9TH, 10TH, 11TH, 12TH, 13TH, 14TH, 15TH, 16TH, 18TH, AND 23RD AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. AS TO OTHER ANSWER, MOTION GRANTED WITH 30 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 2ND (DEFENDANT TO ALLEGE CODE SECTION(S)) AND 4TH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. MOTION DENIED IN ALL OTHER RESPECTS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn To Quash Subpoenas
Off Calendar Apr-13-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Patrick S. Salceda, Esq., for defendants and moving party: Trophy Properties, Citiapartments Inc., The Lembi Group, Frank Lembi and Walter Lembi
Paul Wartelle, Esq., for plaintiff and opposing party: Susan Brown

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendants' Motion To Quash Subpoenas Issued To: Cushman & Wakefield, Alain Pinel, Wells Fargo, Nomura Capital and Credit, Credit Suisse, Hal SF Portfolio, RCC Real Estate, And Resource Real Estate Funding is granted. The requests are overly broad without good cause to invade the right to financial privacy. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Pltf'S Motion To Compel Defts' To Respond To Document Requests, To Produce Documents, And To Produce Andrew Hawkins For Further Deposition Testimony Pursuant To Pltfs' Notice Of Deposition And Demand For Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions;
Off Calendar Apr-13-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Paul Wartelle, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: Susan Brown
Patrick S. Salceda, Esq., for defendants and opposing party: Trophy Properties, Citiapartments Inc., The Lembi Group, Frank Lembi and Walter Lembi

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiffs' Motion To Compel Defendants To Respond To Document Requests, To Produce Documents, And To Produce Andrew Hawkins For Further Deposition Testimony Pursuant To Plaintiffs' Notice Of Deposition And Demand For Production Of Documents is granted in part and denied in part. Andrew Hawkins is ordered to be produced for the resumption of his deposition. This order does not determine that in fact Andrew Hawkins was properly served. However, that objection has been waived by his appearance at the deposition. Mr. Hawkins' objections to the document requests have not been waived. They are objections to be made at the deposition in response to specific requests. However, as his presence waived any objection to his attendance, his presence at the deposition waives his objections to the production of documents based upon the service, but not his objections to documents that are not in his possession and control. Further meet and confer is required as to documents which previously have been produced so that they are identified in advance of the resumption of the deposition.
The deposition shall resume in the Discovery Department. Counsel shall call the Discovery Clerk to set up an afternoon in Room 612 for the resumption of the deposition.
All requests for sanctions are denied. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Mar-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS SUSAN BROWN, DAVID DAVIS, JOSH CEAZAN, and, JOSE ESCOBAR Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings. Appearances: Patrick S. Salceda, Esq. of Gordon & Rees LLP (415) 986-5900, appearing for Defendants and opposing party; Paul Wartelle, Esq. of Wartelle, Weaver & Schreiber (415) 693-0504, appearing for Plaintiff. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted in part, and denied in part. Plaintiff's extrinsic evidence stricken and ruling based on review of defendants' answers only. Motion granted with 30 days leave to amend as to Trophy Properties XV, DE, LLC's 7th, 8th, 9th, 10th, 11th, 12th, 13th, 14th, 15th, 16th, 18th, and 23rd affirmative defenses. As to other answer, motion granted with 30 days leave to amend as to 2nd (defendant to allege code section(s)) and 4th affirmative defenses. Motion denied in all other respects. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
